# s06 sample interactions

# Run 1 (save):
#   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... go run . -v -curator-stale-after 1s "remember my favorite color is blue"

[s06] memory db=/Users/yeding/.learn-hermes-agent/memory.db
[s06] session id=s06-... msgs=1
[s06] plugins=[logging curator]
[s06] tools=[bash memory_save memory_search read_file]
[s06] [plugin:logging] init at 2026-05-09T15:30:00Z
[s06] [plugin:curator] init: stale_after=1s limit=100
[s06] [plugin:logging] session start s06-...
[s06] [plugin:curator] nothing to archive
[turn 0] -> memory_save map[content:user's favorite color is blue tags:[preference]]
[turn 0] <- {"saved":true,"id":1,"tags":[preference]}
[turn 1] assistant: Saved.
Saved.
[s06] [plugin:logging] session end s06-...

# (sleep 2)

# Run 2 (recall — but curator archives the now-stale memory before search):
#   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... go run . -v -curator-stale-after 1s "what's my favorite color?"

[s06] [plugin:curator] archived 1 stale memories: [1]
[turn 0] -> memory_search map[query:favorite color]
[turn 0] <- {"results":[],"note":"no memories matched"}
[turn 1] assistant: I don't have a memory of your favorite color.
I don't have a memory of your favorite color.

# Run 3 (with curator disabled, sleep doesn't matter):
#   go run . -v -no-curator "what's my favorite color?"

[s06] plugins=[logging]
[turn 0] -> memory_search map[query:favorite color]
[turn 0] <- {"results":[{"id":1,"content":"...","archived_at":"..."}]}
# Note: memory_search excludes archived rows, so even with -no-curator
# the search won't find it after a previous run with curator enabled.
# This is by design: archived means "don't surface unless explicitly
# unarchived" — `pin` and `rollback` are upstream-only escape hatches.
